name: Mobile Backend
description: A mobile backend architecture serving native and hybrid mobile applications. The architecture handles unique mobile security challenges including insecure device environments, certificate pinning, offline-first patterns, and push notification delivery. The API gateway serves as the single entry point for all mobile client communication, with backend services processing requests and managing push notification channels.

zones.name: mobile-clients
zones.description: Mobile applications running on user-controlled devices. These devices may be rooted/jailbroken, on compromised networks, or running modified app binaries. Client-side code and local storage can be reverse-engineered and inspected.

zones.name: api-gateway
zones.description: The API gateway layer handling mobile client authentication, app integrity verification, rate limiting, and API version routing. Enforces OAuth 2.0 with PKCE flow designed for public mobile clients.

zones.name: backend-services
zones.description: The backend service tier processing authenticated requests from mobile clients. Handles business logic, data synchronization for offline-first patterns, media processing, and coordinates push notification delivery.

zones.name: data-tier
zones.description: The data persistence layer containing user data, application content, media files, and synchronization state. All data is encrypted at rest and access is restricted to backend services only.

zones.name: push-services
zones.description: External push notification delivery services (Apple APNs, Google FCM) and other notification channels. These are third-party services requiring API credentials and delivering messages through platform-specific channels.

mermaid_template:
flowchart TB
    subgraph mobile["Mobile Clients (Untrusted)"]
        ios[iOS App]
        android[Android App]
        hybrid[Hybrid App]
    end
    subgraph gateway["API Gateway (Semi-Trusted)"]
        gw(API Gateway)
        oauth(OAuth 2.0 + PKCE Server)
        attest(App Attestation)
        version(Version Router)
    end
    subgraph backend["Backend Services (Trusted)"]
        user_svc(User Service)
        biz_svc(Business Logic Service)
        sync_svc(Sync Service)
        media_svc(Media Processor)
        notif(Notification Orchestrator)
    end
    subgraph data["Data Tier (Highly Trusted)"]
        userdb[(User Database)]
        contentdb[(Content Database)]
        media[(Media Storage)]
        syncstate[(Sync State Store)]
    end
    subgraph push["Push Services (Semi-Trusted)"]
        apns(Apple APNs)
        fcm(Google FCM)
        sms(SMS Gateway)
    end
    ios -->|HTTPS + Cert Pin| gw
    android -->|HTTPS + Cert Pin| gw
    hybrid -->|HTTPS + Cert Pin| gw
    gw --> oauth
    gw --> attest
    oauth --> version
    version -->|JWT| user_svc
    version -->|JWT| biz_svc
    version -->|JWT| sync_svc
    version -->|JWT| media_svc
    user_svc -->|SQL/TLS| userdb
    biz_svc -->|SQL/TLS| contentdb
    media_svc -->|S3 API| media
    sync_svc -->|Read/Write| syncstate
    biz_svc --> notif
    notif -->|API Key| apns
    notif -->|API Key| fcm
    notif -->|API Key| sms
